title: passiflora quadriglandulosa var. involucrata
intro: Passiflora quadriglandulosa var. involucrata is a lesser-known species of the Passiflora genus, commonly known as the passionflower plant, native to tropical and subtropical regions. This plant has been traditionally used for its medicinal properties, which include anti-anxiety, antispasmodic, and sedative effects. The health benefits of Passiflora quadriglandulosa var. involucrata include relief from insomnia, anxiety disorders, and menstrual cramps, as well as its potential to lower blood pressure and cholesterol levels. Herbal preparations of this plant can be consumed as teas, tinctures, or infusions, with a typical dosage of 1-3 grams of dried plant material per day.
